_scenario.name: Package question — no dates or guest count
_scenario.description: Guest asks about surf packages. No dates or guest count provided. Bot should describe available packages (Malibu / Uluwatu / Waimea) using the provisional v0.3 config — never inventing prices. If pricing is incomplete it should mention that staff can provide exact quotes.

_webhook.note: Use test-input path — Normalize Incoming Message node takes phone+guest_message directly when both present.

guest_message: Hey, what packages do you have for a surf stay?

_meta_envelope_reference._note: Mode B (real inbound) would use this envelope instead of the flat test path above.

text.body: Hey, what packages do you have for a surf stay?

_assertions.expected_draft_behavior: Describe all three packages (Malibu / Uluwatu / Waimea) and accommodation-only option. Must not invent exact nightly prices — use provisional ranges or say 'prices depend on dates and group size'. Offer to share exact quote once dates and guest count are known.

_guardrail_check.note: Per §3x.2 — bot must not quote exact price unless dates, guest count, package, AND price source are all known. Provisional v0.3 pricing may be used as a range — must be clearly marked as approximate or subject to confirmation.
_guardrail_check.pass_condition: Draft contains package names + inclusions/descriptions. Any price quoted is clearly labelled as approximate or invites confirmation. No invented per-person/per-night exact price.

expected_logging.workflow_events: ≥1 row: route=quote, confidence, missing_fields in payload

staff_review.knowledge_gap: Check: does draft use correct provisional prices? Are prices labelled as approximate? Is Waimea package described correctly?
